creator.name: Anne Minkenberg
creator.expertise: Frontend and Full-Stack development — React Native, FastAPI, Supabase, Expo
creator.background: Anne is building Matchday as a portfolio project to demonstrate full-stack capabilities for job applications. She has already built a working sports betting app (The Kinetic Analyst) with a FastAPI backend, Supabase database, and a polished React Native frontend. She wants to evolve it into a publishable prediction game while adding the technical skills interviewers expect: TypeScript, testing, CI/CD, state management, and custom hooks.

purpose.whoYouHelp: Football fans and sports enthusiasts who follow real-life sporting events and want a fun, competitive way to engage with them beyond just watching
purpose.problemYouSolve: Watching sports is passive. Fans have opinions and predictions but nowhere to test them, prove themselves, or compete with friends in a skill-based, non-monetary way.
purpose.desiredTransformation: From passive spectator to active analyst — users build a reputation as a predictor, climb leaderboards, collect achievements, and challenge friends, all driven by real match outcomes.
purpose.whyYou: Anne built the technical foundation already. She understands the data model (fixtures, odds, results, users, bets), has live API integrations with football data, and has a polished design system. She's transforming a real working app into a game — not starting from scratch.

product.name: Matchday
product.oneLiner: Matchday is a free social prediction game where you compete with friends by calling real football match outcomes.
product.howItWorks: Users get a virtual coin balance and place predictions on upcoming football matches. When matches finish, outcomes are resolved automatically and balances update. Users earn badges for milestones (first correct call, winning streak, etc.), climb a global leaderboard, and can follow friends to see their predictions. The game is tied entirely to real match data fetched from a live football API.
keyCapabilities: Virtual-currency match predictions on real football fixtures with live odds
Automatic result resolution via scheduled backend jobs
Global leaderboard with ranking, win rate, and streak tracking
Friends system — follow users, view their predictions, compare performance
Achievement system with badges triggered by real in-game milestones
product.marketDifferentiation: Unlike real betting apps (Bet365, Betway), Matchday uses virtual currency only — no gambling regulations, no money, no risk. Unlike generic sports trivia apps, every prediction is tied to a real live match happening in the world right now. The game is ephemeral and urgent: this round's fixtures are your playing field.
product.magicMoment: A user places their first prediction on a live match, the final whistle blows, the result is resolved automatically, and they see their balance go up and a badge pop — 'First Correct Call'. They immediately want to see where they rank.

audience.primaryUser: Luca, 26, software developer and football fan. He follows the Bundesliga and Champions League, checks scores on his phone, and has running bets with friends about match outcomes via WhatsApp. He'd love a proper place to track predictions, prove he's right, and flex his record — but doesn't want to spend real money.
secondaryUsers: Casual football fans who want a low-stakes, game-like way to get more invested in matches they're already watching
Friend groups who want a shared competitive activity during football season — a group to join and compare picks
Developers and recruiters reviewing Anne's portfolio — the app demonstrates real-world full-stack skills
audience.currentAlternatives: WhatsApp group prediction threads (no tracking, no history), Kickbase/fantasy football (complex, time-heavy), real sports betting apps (money required, addictive), or just not playing at all.
audience.frustrations: WhatsApp threads are chaotic and untracked. Fantasy football requires too much weekly management. Real betting apps create financial risk and aren't fun for casual engagement. There's no clean, social, zero-stakes prediction game built around real fixtures.

business.initialGoal: Complete app with all core game mechanics working, TypeScript fully integrated, unit and integration tests written for both frontend and backend, CI/CD pipeline live on GitHub Actions — strong enough to demo in a Full-Stack dev interview.
business.sixMonthVision: App published to GitHub with a public demo, documented architecture, and a README that clearly communicates technical decisions. Potentially submitted to the App Store as a portfolio piece.
business.constraints: Solo developer, portfolio project. No budget for paid APIs beyond free tiers. Time-constrained — needs to be interview-ready. No prior TypeScript experience but keen to learn. No prior testing experience — needs to establish patterns.
business.goToMarket: GitHub portfolio + README as primary showcase. Code quality, architecture decisions, and the PLAID docs themselves demonstrate product thinking. No marketing needed.

feeling.brandPersonality: Precision meets energy. Like a Bloomberg Terminal crossed with a stadium atmosphere — sharp, data-forward, but with the pulse of live sport underneath. Confident without being arrogant.
feeling.visualMood: Dark analytics terminal: near-black backgrounds (#10141a), cyan primary glow (#00f0ff), purple secondary (#9b59ff), tonal card layering instead of borders. Feels like a live data feed, not a game show.
feeling.toneOfVoice: Clipped and direct, like a sports analyst. 'MATCH FEED. Live Markets.' Not chatty. Labels in uppercase, data in high contrast. Microcopy is terse and confident. Errors are factual: 'Insufficient balance' not 'Oops, something went wrong!'
feeling.antiPatterns: Never casual or emoji-heavy. Never bright-white or pastel — the dark aesthetic is core to the brand. Never slow or unresponsive — sport is live, the app must feel live. Never feel like a gambling product — no 'PLACE YOUR BETS' language, always 'MAKE YOUR CALL' or 'PREDICTION'.

frontend.rationale: Already the basis of the existing app. Migration from JSX to TSX is the primary technical goal. Expo simplifies builds and OTA updates. TypeScript adds strict typing for API responses, navigation params, and component props — the key portfolio skill gap to close.

backend.rationale: Already implemented with route/service/model separation, Pydantic models, JWT auth middleware, and APScheduler cron jobs. Pytest will be added for unit and integration tests.

database.rationale: Already in use for users, matches, bets. New tables needed for: friendships, achievements, user_achievements, leaderboard_snapshots. Supabase RLS policies will be documented as a full-stack skill signal.

auth.rationale: Already integrated with JWT tokens passed to FastAPI middleware. Email/password + magic link already implemented on LoginScreen and RegisterScreen.

payments.rationale: No payments. Virtual currency only — balance managed in the users table in Supabase.
